design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore level → find switches/keys → defeat monsters → reach exit → progress to next level. Cycle repeats across 4 episodes with hub-based episode selection.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Combat against escalating monster threats in increasingly complex architectural environments; exploration tension through hidden secrets and optional difficulty paths
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Armed soldier fighting through interdimensional invasion combining science-fiction military bases with gothic/Lovecraftian fantasy settings; wielding diverse sci-fi and fantasy weaponry
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Merges Doom's proven deathmatch multiplayer with full real-time 3D rendering; deliberately blends sci-fi and gothic fantasy aesthetics rather than committing to one; Nightmare difficulty explicitly designed to gatekeep players ('hidden so people won't wander in by accident'); hub-based episode structure where player collects runes rather than linear progression

difficulty.continues_system: Level restart on death; items reset per episode in campaign mode
difficulty.checkpointing: Free saving between levels in console versions; anytime saving in PC versions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Four distinct skill levels presented before each episode (Easy, Medium, Hard, and hidden Nightmare); players can freely choose difficulty per episode; Nightmare intentionally obscured to prevent accidental selection

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon and item collection during levels (double-barreled shotgun, sci-fi and fantasy weapons); items lost on death in multiplayer; items reset when starting new episode in campaign
progression.unlock_mechanism: Keys and switches open doors within levels; four runes (one per episode) unlock final END level containing puzzle; episode hub (START level) gating
replayability_hooks: Secret areas within each level encourage exploration
One secret level per episode (including special low-gravity challenge level)
Four distinct difficulty modes with different monster placements/quantities
Multiplayer deathmatch modes as alternative to campaign

world_building_techniques: Environmental contrast: futuristic military bases vs. medieval gothic architecture
Enemy design reflecting setting: possessed soldiers in bases, demonic beasts and armored knights in fantasy sections
Weapon variety: sci-fi armaments in bases, fantasy-inspired weapons in gothic areas
Lovecraftian visual language and aesthetic throughout environments

boss_design.philosophy: Episode-focused encounters; boss appears at end of Episode 1 (traditional structure); Episode 2-4 emphasize escalating monster encounters and architectural challenges over discrete boss fights
notable_bosses: Episode 1 final boss (unspecified in source, traditional boss structure confirmed)

faction_indicators: Military faction: futuristic soldier protagonist, military base environments
Demonic/Lovecraftian faction: gothic architecture, demonic enemies, magical runes
danger_signals: Escalating monster difficulties tied to skill level selection
environmental_storytelling: Military base destruction and corruption indicates invasion progression
Transition from sci-fi bases to gothic fantasy implies dimensional rifts
Rune collection suggests ritual/magical significance to invasion
Hidden Nightmare difficulty level signals hidden threats
